title: New Territories

A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing Chinese Wikipedia article at [[:zh:新界]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|zh|新界}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:New_Territories "Talk:New Territories"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation").

A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing Chinese Wikipedia article at [[:zh:新界]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|zh|新界}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:New_Territories "Talk:New Territories"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation"). |

is the largest of the three [areas of Hong Kong](/wiki/Areas_of_Hong_Kong "Areas of Hong Kong"), alongside [Hong Kong Island](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Island "Hong Kong Island") and [Kowloon](/wiki/Kowloon "Kowloon"). It makes up 86.2% of Hong Kong's territory, and contains around half of the population of Hong Kong. Historically, it is the region described in the [Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory](/wiki/Convention_for_the_Extension_of_Hong_Kong_Territory "Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory"). According to that treaty, the territories comprise the mainland area north of [Boundary Street](/wiki/Boundary_Street "Boundary Street") on the Kowloon Peninsula and south of the [Sham Chun River](/wiki/Sham_Chun_River "Sham Chun River") (which is the border between Hong Kong and [mainland China](/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China")), as well as over 200 [outlying islands](/wiki/Outlying_Islands,_Hong_Kong "Outlying Islands, Hong Kong"), including [Lantau Island](/wiki/Lantau_Island "Lantau Island"), [Lamma Island](/wiki/Lamma_Island "Lamma Island"), [Cheung Chau](/wiki/Cheung_Chau "Cheung Chau"), and [Peng Chau](/wiki/Peng_Chau "Peng Chau") in the territory of Hong Kong.

For the Eric Lau album, see [New Territories (album)](/wiki/New_Territories_(album) "New Territories (album)"). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Area of Hong Kong

This article is about the area of Hong Kong. For the community in Houston, see [New Territory, Sugar Land, Texas](/wiki/New_Territory,_Sugar_Land,_Texas "New Territory, Sugar Land, Texas"). For the Eric Lau album, see [New Territories (album)](/wiki/New_Territories_(album) "New Territories (album)"). |  | |

The **New Territories** is the largest of the three [areas of Hong Kong](/wiki/Areas_of_Hong_Kong "Areas of Hong Kong"), alongside [Hong Kong Island](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Island "Hong Kong Island") and [Kowloon](/wiki/Kowloon "Kowloon"). It makes up 86.2% of Hong Kong's territory, and contains around half of the population of Hong Kong. Historically, it is the region described in the [Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory](/wiki/Convention_for_the_Extension_of_Hong_Kong_Territory "Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory"). According to that treaty, the territories comprise the mainland area north of [Boundary Street](/wiki/Boundary_Street "Boundary Street") on the Kowloon Peninsula and south of the [Sham Chun River](/wiki/Sham_Chun_River "Sham Chun River") (which is the border between Hong Kong and [mainland China](/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China")), as well as over 200 [outlying islands](/wiki/Outlying_Islands,_Hong_Kong "Outlying Islands, Hong Kong"), including [Lantau Island](/wiki/Lantau_Island "Lantau Island"), [Lamma Island](/wiki/Lamma_Island "Lamma Island"), [Cheung Chau](/wiki/Cheung_Chau "Cheung Chau"), and [Peng Chau](/wiki/Peng_Chau "Peng Chau") in the territory of Hong Kong.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/Butterfly_Bay%2C_Tuen_Mun%2C_NT%2C_Hong_Kong_in_2017.jpg/250px-Butterfly_Bay%2C_Tuen_Mun%2C_NT%2C_Hong_Kong_in_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Butterfly_Bay,_Tuen_Mun,_NT,_Hong_Kong_in_2017.jpg)

Butterfly Bay in New Territories

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/HKRegionalCouncilFlag.svg/250px-HKRegionalCouncilFlag.svg.png)](/wiki/File:HKRegionalCouncilFlag.svg)

Flag of the [New Territories Regional Council](/wiki/Regional_Council_(Hong_Kong) "Regional Council (Hong Kong)"), which was disbanded on the last day of 1999

Later, after [New Kowloon](/wiki/New_Kowloon "New Kowloon") was defined from the area between the Boundary Street and the Kowloon Ranges spanned from [Lai Chi Kok](/wiki/Lai_Chi_Kok "Lai Chi Kok") to [Lei Yue Mun](/wiki/Lei_Yue_Mun "Lei Yue Mun"), and the extension of the urban areas of Kowloon, New Kowloon was gradually urbanised and absorbed into Kowloon.

The New Territories now comprises only the mainland north of the Kowloon Ranges and south of the Sham Chun River, as well as the Outlying Islands. It comprises an area of 952 square kilometres (368 sq mi).[[1]](#cite_note-britannica-1) Nevertheless, New Kowloon has remained statutorily part of the New Territories instead of Kowloon.

The New Territories were leased from [Qing China](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty") by the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland") in 1898 for 99 years in the [Second Convention of Peking](/wiki/Second_Convention_of_Peking "Second Convention of Peking") (The Convention for the Extension of Hong Kong Territory). Upon the expiry of the lease, [sovereignty was transferred](/wiki/Transfer_of_sovereignty_over_Hong_Kong "Transfer of sovereignty over Hong Kong") to the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China") in 1997, together with the Qing-ceded territories of Hong Kong Island and Kowloon Peninsula.

In 2021, the population of the New Territories was recorded at 3,984,077,[[2]](#cite_note-census2021-2) with a population density of 4,140 per square kilometre (10,720/sq mi).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Lease of New Territories

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Lease of New Territories")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cf/1900_Map_of_the_Colony_of_Hong_Kong.jpg/250px-1900_Map_of_the_Colony_of_Hong_Kong.jpg)](/wiki/File:1900_Map_of_the_Colony_of_Hong_Kong.jpg)

1900 map of Hong Kong and Kowloon with the New Territories acquired by the British in 1898

[Hong Kong Island](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Island "Hong Kong Island") was ceded to [Britain](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland") in 1842 by the [Treaty of Nanjing](/wiki/Treaty_of_Nanjing "Treaty of Nanjing") ending the [First Opium War](/wiki/First_Opium_War "First Opium War"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) and [Kowloon](/wiki/Kowloon "Kowloon") south of [Boundary Street](/wiki/Boundary_Street "Boundary Street") and [Stonecutters Island](/wiki/Stonecutters_Island "Stonecutters Island") were ceded in 1860 by a lease[[4]](#cite_note-Parkes-1860-4) subsequently incorporated into the [Convention of Beijing](/wiki/Convention_of_Beijing "Convention of Beijing") ending the [Second Opium War](/wiki/Second_Opium_War "Second Opium War").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The colony of Hong Kong attracted a large number of Chinese and Westerners to seek their fortune in the city. Its population increased rapidly and the city became overcrowded. The outbreak of [bubonic plague](/wiki/Bubonic_plague "Bubonic plague") in 1894 became a concern to the [Hong Kong government](/wiki/Hong_Kong_government "Hong Kong government"). There was a need to expand the colony to accommodate its growing population. The [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty")'s defeat in the [First Sino-Japanese War](/wiki/First_Sino-Japanese_War "First Sino-Japanese War") had shown that it was incapable of defending itself. [Victoria City](/wiki/Victoria,_Hong_Kong "Victoria, Hong Kong") and [Victoria Harbour](/wiki/Victoria_Harbour "Victoria Harbour") were vulnerable to any hostile forces launching attacks from the hills of Kowloon.

Alarmed by the encroachment of other European powers in China, Britain also feared for the security of Hong Kong. In June 1898, using the [most favoured nation](/wiki/Most_favoured_nation "Most favoured nation") clause that it had previously secured from China, the United Kingdom demanded and received an extension of Kowloon[[6]](#cite_note-hkextension-6) to counter the establishment of [France](/wiki/Third_French_Republic "Third French Republic")'s new [Guangzhouwan](/wiki/Guangzhouwan "Guangzhouwan") colony, which was well reinforced by their control of nearby [Indochina](/wiki/French_Indochina "French Indochina"). In similar fashion, in July, it secured [Weihaiwei](/wiki/Weihaiwei_under_British_rule "Weihaiwei under British rule") in Shandong in the north as a base for operations against the Germans in [Jiaozhou Bay](/wiki/Jiaozhou_Bay_Leased_Territory "Jiaozhou Bay Leased Territory") ([Qingdao](/wiki/Qingdao "Qingdao")) and the Russians in [Liaodong](/wiki/Russian_Dalian "Russian Dalian") ([Dalian](/wiki/Dalian "Dalian")). Chinese officials stayed in the walled cities of [Kowloon City](/wiki/Kowloon_Walled_City "Kowloon Walled City") and Weihaiwei.

The extension of Kowloon was called the New Territories. The additional land was estimated to be 365 square miles (950 km2) or 12 times the size of the existing [colonial Hong Kong](/wiki/Colonial_Hong_Kong "Colonial Hong Kong") at the time.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[*[verification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*]

### British assumption of sovereignty

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: British assumption of sovereignty")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/New_Territories "Special:EditPage/New Territories") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *(October 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/British_take_over_the_New_Territories.jpg/250px-British_take_over_the_New_Territories.jpg)](/wiki/File:British_take_over_the_New_Territories.jpg)

The British ceremony in Tai Po, 1899, assuming control of the New Territories

Although the convention was signed on the 9 June 1898 and became effective on 1 July, the British did not take over the New Territories immediately. During this period, there was no [Hong Kong governor](/wiki/Hong_Kong_governor "Hong Kong governor") and [Wilsone Black](/wiki/Wilsone_Black "Wilsone Black") acted as administrator. [James Stewart Lockhart](/wiki/James_Stewart_Lockhart "James Stewart Lockhart"), the [colonial secretary of Hong Kong](/wiki/Colonial_Secretary_of_Hong_Kong "Colonial Secretary of Hong Kong"), was sent back from England to make a survey of New Territories before formal transfer. The survey found that the new frontier at Sham Chun River (Shenzhen river) suggested by Wilsone Black was far from ideal. It excluded the town of [Shenzhen](/wiki/Shenzhen "Shenzhen") (Sham Chun), and the boundary would divide the town. There was no mountain range as a natural border. Lockhart suggested moving the frontier to the line of hills north of Shenzhen. This suggestion was not received favourably and the Chinese official suggested the frontier be moved to the hill much further south of the Sham Chun River. It was settled in March 1899 that the boundary remain at the Sham Chun River.

The new Hong Kong governor, [Henry Blake](/wiki/Henry_Arthur_Blake "Henry Arthur Blake"), arrived in November 1898. The date for the takeover of the New Territories was fixed as 17 April 1899, and [Tai Po](/wiki/Tai_Po "Tai Po") was chosen as the administrative centre. The transfer was not smooth and peaceful. In early April 1899, the captain superintendent of police, [Francis Henry May](/wiki/Francis_Henry_May "Francis Henry May"), and some policemen erected a flagstaff and temporary headquarters at Tai Po and posted the governor's proclamation of the takeover date. Fearing for their traditional land rights, in the [Six-Day War of 1899](/wiki/Six-Day_War_(1899) "Six-Day War (1899)"), a number of clans attempted to resist the British, mobilising clan militias[[8]](#cite_note-8) that had been organised and armed to protect against longshore raids by pirates. The militia men attempted a frontal attack against the temporary police station in Tai Po that was the main British base but were beaten back by superior force of arms. An attempt by the clansmen at guerilla warfare was put down by the British near [Lam Tsuen](/wiki/Lam_Tsuen "Lam Tsuen") with over 500 Chinese men killed, and collapsed when British artillery was brought to bear on the [walled villages](/wiki/Walled_villages_of_Hong_Kong "Walled villages of Hong Kong") of the clansmen. Most prominent of the villages in the resistance [Kat Hing Wai](/wiki/Kat_Hing_Wai "Kat Hing Wai"), of the [Tang clan](/wiki/Tang_clan "Tang clan"), was symbolically disarmed, by having its main gates dismounted and removed. However, in order to prevent future resistance the British made concessions to the [indigenous inhabitants](/wiki/Indigenous_inhabitants_of_the_New_Territories_(Hong_Kong) "Indigenous inhabitants of the New Territories (Hong Kong)") with regard to land use, land inheritance and marriage laws; the majority of which remained in place into the 1960s when polygamy was outlawed. Some of the concessions with regard to land use and inheritance remain in place in Hong Kong to this day and is a source of friction between indigenous inhabitants and other Hong Kong residents.

[Lord Lugard](/wiki/Frederick_Lugard "Frederick Lugard") was governor from 1907 to 1912, and he proposed the return of [Weihaiwei](/wiki/Weihaiwei_under_British_rule "Weihaiwei under British rule") to the Chinese government, in return for the ceding of the leased New Territories in perpetuity. The proposal was not received favourably, although if it had been acted on, Hong Kong might have remained forever in British hands.

### New town development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: New town development")]

Main article: [New towns of Hong Kong](/wiki/New_towns_of_Hong_Kong "New towns of Hong Kong")

Much of the New Territories was, and to a limited extent still is, made up of rural areas. Attempts at modernising the area did not become fully committed until the late 1970s, when many [new towns](/wiki/New_town#Hong_Kong "New town") were built to accommodate the population growth from urbanised areas of Kowloon and Hong Kong Island. Despite rapid development of the new towns, which now accommodate a population of over 3 million, the [Hong Kong government](/wiki/Hong_Kong_government "Hong Kong government") confines built-up areas to a few areas and reserves large parts of the region as [parkland](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Country_Parks "Hong Kong Country Parks").

### Sovereignty transfer to the PRC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sovereignty transfer to the PRC")]

As the expiry date of the lease neared in the 1980s, talks between the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") and the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China") led to the signing of the [Sino-British Joint Declaration](/wiki/Sino-British_Joint_Declaration "Sino-British Joint Declaration") (1984), in which the whole of Hong Kong would be returned, instead of only the New Territories.

## Districts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Districts")]

The New Territories comprises nine [districts](/wiki/Districts_of_Hong_Kong "Districts of Hong Kong"), each with their own [district council](/wiki/District_councils_of_Hong_Kong "District councils of Hong Kong"):

* [New Territories East](/wiki/New_Territories_East "New Territories East")
  + [North District](/wiki/North_District,_Hong_Kong "North District, Hong Kong")
  + [Sai Kung District](/wiki/Sai_Kung_District "Sai Kung District")
  + [Sha Tin District](/wiki/Sha_Tin_District "Sha Tin District")
  + [Tai Po District](/wiki/Tai_Po_District "Tai Po District")
* [New Territories West](/wiki/New_Territories_West "New Territories West")
  + [Islands District](/wiki/Islands_District,_Hong_Kong "Islands District, Hong Kong")
  + [Kwai Tsing District](/wiki/Kwai_Tsing_District "Kwai Tsing District") ([Kwai Chung](/wiki/Kwai_Chung "Kwai Chung") and [Tsing Yi Island](/wiki/Tsing_Yi "Tsing Yi"))
  + [Tsuen Wan District](/wiki/Tsuen_Wan_District "Tsuen Wan District")
  + [Tuen Mun District](/wiki/Tuen_Mun_District "Tuen Mun District")
  + [Yuen Long District](/wiki/Yuen_Long_District "Yuen Long District")

## Population

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Population")]

According to the [2021 census](/wiki/Census_in_Hong_Kong "Census in Hong Kong"), the population of the New Territories was 3,984,077, representing 53.7% of Hong Kong's total population. A total of 90.4% of the residents of New Territories use [Cantonese](/wiki/Cantonese_language "Cantonese language") as their main language. Meanwhile, 3.5% of its residents use [English](/wiki/English_language "English language"), 2.0% use [Mandarin Chinese](/wiki/Mandarin_Chinese "Mandarin Chinese"), and 2.3% use other Chinese dialects. A total of 93.1% the district's population is of [Chinese](/wiki/Han_Chinese "Han Chinese") descent. The largest ethnic minority groups are [Filipinos](/wiki/Filipinos "Filipinos") (2.1%), [Indonesians](/wiki/Indonesians "Indonesians") (1.8%), [South Asians](/wiki/South_Asian_ethnic_groups "South Asian ethnic groups") (1.1%), [Mixed](/wiki/Mixed_ethnicity "Mixed ethnicity") (0.8%) and [Whites](/wiki/White_people "White people") (0.7%).[[2]](#cite_note-census2021-2)

## New Kowloon

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_Territories&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: New Kowloon")]

[New Kowloon](/wiki/New_Kowloon "New Kowloon") covers the entirety of the [Wong Tai Sin](/wiki/Wong_Tai_Sin_District "Wong Tai Sin District") and [Kwun Tong](/wiki/Kwun_Tong_District "Kwun Tong District") districts, as well as the mainland portion of the [Sham Shui Po District](/wiki/Sham_Shui_Po_District "Sham Shui Po District") (i.e. excluding the [Stonecutters Island](/wiki/Stonecutters_Island "Stonecutters Island")) and the northern portion of the [Kowloon City District](/wiki/Kowloon_City_District "Kowloon City District") (portion to the north of Boundary Street/Prince Edward Road West[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*], as well as reclaimed land including the [Kai Tak Airport](/wiki/Kai_Tak_Airport "Kai Tak Airport")).
